questions.question: Electric current is defined as the:
options: Amount of energy per unit charge
Rate of flow of electric charge
Opposition to the flow of charge
Total charge stored in a battery
questions.explanation: Current is the rate at which charge flows past a point — how many coulombs per second pass through.

questions.question: The correct formula for electric current is:
questions.explanation: $I = Q/t$, where $I$ is current in amperes, $Q$ is charge in coulombs, and $t$ is time in seconds.

questions.question: The SI unit of electric current is the:
questions.explanation: Current is measured in amperes (A). One ampere equals one coulomb of charge per second.

questions.question: A charge of $30\text{ C}$ passes through a wire in $6.0\text{ s}$. What is the current?
A current of $4.0\text{ A}$ flows through a circuit for $10\text{ s}$. How much charge passes a point in the circuit?
A current of $2.5\text{ A}$ delivers $50\text{ C}$ of charge. How long did the current flow?
Conventional current flows from:
options: Random direction depending on the circuit
questions.explanation: Conventional current is defined as the flow of positive charge — from the positive terminal to the negative terminal.

questions.question: In a circuit, electrons actually flow from:
options: The same direction as conventional current
The center of the wire outward
questions.explanation: Electrons (negative charge) are repelled by the negative terminal and attracted to the positive terminal. They flow opposite to conventional current.

questions.question: To measure the current through a component, an ammeter must be connected:
questions.explanation: An ammeter measures current flowing through it, so it must be placed in series — the current must pass through the meter.

questions.question: In a series circuit, the current is:
questions.explanation: A series circuit has only one path. The current is identical everywhere in the loop.

questions.question: In a parallel circuit, the total current is equal to:
options: The current in any single branch
The sum of the currents in all branches
questions.explanation: Current splits at junctions in parallel. The total current from the source equals the sum of the branch currents.

questions.question: A current of $350$ milliamperes is equal to:

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered electric current and circuit fundamentals!
scoreMessages.70: Good work! Review conventional current vs. electron flow and ammeter connection.
scoreMessages.50: Keep practicing! Focus on $I = Q/t$ and current behavior in series vs. parallel circuits.
scoreMessages.0: Review the basics: definition of current, unit (ampere), formula $I = Q/t$, and series vs. parallel current.
